description: Dissident Irish republican paramilitary group formed in 1997 after a split from the Provisional IRA, aiming to create a united Ireland. It waged a campaign in Northern Ireland against British security forces and carried out bombings, most notably the 1998 Omagh bombing. The group merged into the New IRA in 2012, though some remnants remain active.

country_links.evidence_quote: three members travelled to Split in Croatia to purchase arms, which were smuggled back to Ireland
country_links.context: The Real IRA purchased arms from Croatia and smuggled them back to Ireland.

country_links.evidence_quote: The RIRA had attempted to procure arms from France including Semtex and C-4 plastic explosives...
country_links.context: The RIRA attempted to procure arms from France.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2011 Michael Campbell, brother of Liam, was found guilty in Vilnius, Lithuania, of trying to purchase arms and explosives...
country_links.context: A RIRA member was found guilty in Lithuania of trying to purchase arms and explosives.

country_links.evidence_quote: ...while the other was sentenced to four years' imprisonment for making a Portuguese property available for the purpose of terrorism.
country_links.context: A RIRA member made a Portuguese property available for the purpose of terrorism.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2001, RIRA members travelled to Slovakia to procure arms...
country_links.context: RIRA members travelled to Slovakia to procure arms.

country_links.evidence_quote: Five RIRA members were also convicted in connection with the 2001 bombing campaign in England...
country_links.context: The RIRA carried out a bombing campaign in England and attacked British security forces in Northern Ireland.

country_links.evidence_quote: designated a proscribed terrorist organisation in the ... United States
country_links.context: The Real IRA was designated a proscribed terrorist organisation in the United States.
